llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: Single AI treatment arm where participants were encouraged to use ChatGPT. This is a between-subjects design with random assignment to treatment (encouraged AI use) vs control (discouraged AI use).
llm_extracted.notes: Table S1 Panel A provides the baseline constant (314.465 seconds, SE=7.554) which represents the control group mean for email task completion time. The regression coefficient (-134.010, SE=14.548) represents the treatment effect. With both the baseline mean and the regression coefficient in levels, we can compute log(1 + coef/baseline) = log(1 + (-134.010/314.465)) = log(0.574). The outcome is time in seconds (top-coded at 900), so smaller is better. The paper reports this as a 2SLS/LATE estimate where treatment assignment instruments for actual AI use.
source_quotes: Used AI −134.010*** (14.548) Constant 314.465*** (7.554) for Email Time in Table S1 Panel A: Second stage
We estimated the local average treatment effect (LATE) of using generative AI with a two-stage least squares (2SLS) procedure
Time (in seconds) respondents spent on each of the tasks. Since some respondents spent a large amount of time on a single task, we top-coded the time measure for each task at 900 seconds
Predicted time savings range between 101 and 235 seconds (0.54 to 1.04 standard deviations (SD))

computed.notes: Table S1 Panel A provides the baseline constant (314.465 seconds, SE=7.554) which represents the control group mean for email task completion time. The regression coefficient (-134.010, SE=14.548) represents the treatment effect. With both the baseline mean and the regression coefficient in levels, we can compute log(1 + coef/baseline) = log(1 + (-134.010/314.465)) = log(0.574). The outcome is time in seconds (top-coded at 900), so smaller is better. The paper reports this as a 2SLS/LATE estimate where treatment assignment instruments for actual AI use.
